prompt:
Our metrics agents ship counter deltas over UDP and we're paying for every byte, so we're moving the payload to LEB128 varints with zigzag for the signed deltas. I need the codec crate. The package (rs-varint) is already set up with the acceptance tests in tests/varint.rs — write src/lib.rs; don't touch tests/, Cargo.toml, or .gitignore, and we're stdlib-only, no crates.io.

Wire format, unsigned: standard base-128 varint — 7 payload bits per byte little-endian (least-significant group first), high bit = continuation. u64 caps at 10 bytes; in the 10th byte only the lowest payload bit may be used. Signed values are zigzag-mapped first (0→0, -1→1, 1→2, -2→3, ... i64::MIN→u64::MAX) and then encoded as unsigned. Examples we must match exactly: 300 → [0xAC, 0x02], u64::MAX → nine 0xFF then 0x01.

Decoding is strict because these frames cross a trust boundary: (a) a frame that ends mid-varint is an error, (b) non-canonical (overlong) encodings are errors — if the varint is more than one byte long and its final byte is 0x00 it carries dead padding and gets rejected (so [0x81,0x00] is NOT a legal 1; a ten-byte all-zero-payload varint is overlong, not overflow), (c) anything that doesn't fit in u64 — 10th byte with the continuation bit still set, or with payload bits above the lowest — is an overflow error. Every error must say WHERE: carry the byte offset of the FIRST byte of the offending varint.

Public API (exact):
- pub enum DecodeError { Truncated { start: usize }, Overlong { start: usize }, Overflow { start: usize } } — deriving Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq; implements Display (messages exactly "truncated varint starting at byte N", "overlong varint encoding starting at byte N", "varint overflows u64 starting at byte N") and std::error::Error.
- pub fn zigzag_encode(value: i64) -> u64 and pub fn zigzag_decode(value: u64) -> i64.
- pub fn encode_u64(value: u64, out: &mut Vec<u8>) and pub fn encode_i64(value: i64, out: &mut Vec<u8>) — append to the buffer.
- pub struct Decoder<'a> over a &'a [u8] with Decoder::new(buf), position(&self) -> usize (absolute byte offset), remaining(&self) -> usize, decode_u64(&mut self) -> Result<Option<u64>, DecodeError>, decode_i64(&mut self) -> Result<Option<i64>, DecodeError>. Clean end of buffer = Ok(None). On success the decoder advances past the varint; on error it must NOT consume anything — position() stays at the start of the bad varint and calling decode again returns the same error.
- pub fn decode_all_u64(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u64>, DecodeError> and pub fn decode_all_i64(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<i64>, DecodeError> — whole-stream decode, first error wins.

Acceptance: cargo test --offline goes green. The tests pin the byte images, the zigzag table, decoder positions after every value, and the exact error variant + offset for truncated/overlong/overflow frames — treat them as the spec.
